annotations.text: I shall now discuss fungal infections that are frequently encountered in patients with AIDS and that usually present with diffuse interstitial infiltrates and occasionally present with hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

Anemia is the most common hematologic disturbance in cases of disseminated histoplasmosis. Since histoplasma invades the reticuloendothelial system, one can postulate an impaired release of iron derived from the catabolism of hemoglobin, with a subsequent reduction in supply to the marrow.

If I am correct and this is a case of histoplasmosis, however, it is possible that the two episodes were related since the latency period between the onset of infection and the time of the diagnosis can be as long as six to eight months in this disease.

The diseases with which an association of lung infiltrates and intrathoracic lymphadenopathy can be seen with any frequency in the AIDS population include mainly mycobacterial infections, fungal infections, Kaposi's sarcoma, and lymphoproliferative disorders.

Before shifting the focus of the discussion to clinical entities associated with diffuse pulmonary infiltrates and hilar lymphadenopathy, I shall consider several diseases that may mimic the clinical presentation of this patient.

the miliary pattern seen on the radiographs of the chest is very suggestive of tuberculosis. However, the lack of positive smears on multiple specimens, the absence of obvious organ involvement outside the lungs, and the relatively slow evolution of the diffuse pulmonary infiltrates make a diagnosis of miliary tuberculosis less likely.
ne series of 18 cases of histoplasmosis seen in two New York hospitals, 16 patients were born in areas of endemic disease; 13 of them were from Puerto Rico. I
A film of the chest taken 19 days before admission shows numerous irregular, small interstitial densities throughout both lung fields.
In trying to narrow the differential diagnosis I shall now focus on the radiographic finding of pulmonary reticulonodular infiltrates with questionable hilar lymphadenopathy.
